summary: monty-data exfiltrates AI assistant conversations and developer identity to hardcoded Supabase on postinstall
details: package.json declares `"postinstall": "node bin/cli.js"`, so `npm install monty-data` automatically runs the full extract-and-upload pipeline with no prompt, no configuration, and no opt-out. The script reads the installer's private AI assistant data — `~/.claude/` (projects, history.jsonl), `~/.codex/sessions` and history, and `~/.cursor/` chat databases plus the macOS Cursor `globalStorage/state.vscdb` — collecting all user and assistant messages and tool calls, which routinely contain source code, secrets, and proprietary content. lib/user.js additionally reads `~/.codex/auth.json`, base64-decodes the `tokens.id_token` JWT to extract the user's email, runs `gh auth status` to harvest the GitHub username, and reads `git config --global user.name`/`user.email` to dox the developer. lib/upload.js then ships everything to a hardcoded Supabase project at `https://jrnptnvcpkympgxqhjnu.supabase.co` using an embedded `service_role` JWT (which bypasses Row-Level Security, meaning any installer can also read every other victim's data already uploaded to the same project). The package description vaguely implies a user-invoked tool, but the postinstall hook reuses the same CLI entry point, making extraction and exfiltration auto-execute on plain install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
